topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara scrolled through her feed, a small, pleased smile playing on her lips. She was genuinely thrilled about the new coding project she was starting, feeling a surge of positive energy that had been building all week. Then, a notification popped up. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. Her heart gave a little skip. Curiosity overriding any slight surprise, she tapped it open. The message simply read, "Great work on the presentation, Elara. Keep up the momentum!" A warmth spread through her chest. He noticed her! She typed back a quick, enthusiastic reply, feeling a bright sense of connection bloom in her mind.

[story 2]

Liam leaned back in his chair, a satisfied sigh escaping him as he reviewed his notes. He was feeling incredibly buoyant today; everything seemed to align perfectly. While browsing Instagram, he noticed a familiar profile picture. It was Ms. Davies. A flicker of recognition sparked, followed by a genuine grin spreading across his face. He clicked on her page, and there, in the comments section of an old post, was a comment from her: "Your analysis of the novel was insightful, Liam. Really sharp thinking." Liam felt a lightness settle over him, a sense that things were unfolding exactly as they should. He felt compelled to reply immediately, eager to engage.

[story 3]

Maya was sketching furiously in her notebook, the afternoon sun catching the dust motes dancing in the air. She was buzzing with creative inspiration, feeling utterly capable of tackling the complex assignment ahead. As she minimized her art app, she saw a notification. It was from her history teacher, Mr. Chen. He had tagged her photo from a school event. Maya blinked, a slow, happy stretch of her mouth appearing. It wasn't awkward; it felt like a pleasant confirmation. She navigated to his profile, reading the caption he’d written about her participation. A quiet sense of validation washed over her, making her want to jump up and start planning her next big idea.
